description: Shared correctness fixtures (docs/benchmark-correctness-fixtures.md). Deterministic synthetic latency streams (xorshift64 + integer bimodal mapping) recorded into HdrHistogram-compatible histograms (1 ns..60 s, 3 significant digits). Both languages must reproduce every value below exactly — sequences via identical integer arithmetic, percentiles via the shared HdrHistogram bucket semantics (Java org.hdrhistogram, Rust hdrhistogram crate). Histogram memory footprint is implementation-specific and deliberately NOT a shared fixture. Change a value here only together with both test files.

semantics.xorshift64: x ^= x << 13; x ^= x >>> 7; x ^= x << 17 (64-bit wrapping, logical right shift)
semantics.bimodalNanos: u = stream value (unsigned): u%100 < 95 -> 800 + u%400; else 40000 + u%20000
semantics.pauseInjection: bimodal sequence with +5000000 ns added at indices 999, 1999, ... (every 1000th op)
semantics.checksum: checksum*31 + value, 64-bit wrapping, over the full sequence
semantics.histogram: lowest=1, highest=60000000000, significantDigits=3; corrected recording uses expectedInterval=1000 ns
semantics.merge: two independently recorded histograms added into a fresh one
semantics.burstResponse: single-server FIFO: bursts of 10 arrivals every 50000 ns; arrival_i=(i/10)*50000; completion=max(arrival,prevCompletion)+service; response=completion-arrival (service from the bimodal stream)

p999.note: p99.9 falls EXACTLY on the stall cliff (100 stalls / 100000 values); the two HdrHistogram implementations resolve the tie differently. Documented instrument difference — excluded from cross-language pinning; all off-boundary percentiles match exactly. Taught in benchmark.md as the percentile-convention trap.
